rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY CHONG KWONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom use the Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 and Password: 514879 ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Still no response to Examiner notes. At the last hearing counsel indicated attempting to obtain waivers. No new filings have been made. Court to determine how to proceed and whether to OSC sanctions. 2) Examiner notes: a. Since 04/18/2022 the tentative ruling has indicated the 2-year account must begin with Decedent's date of death. Decedent died 07/08/2009. This account begins 10/01/2019. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.34]. Unless otherwise permitted for good cause, if an account exceeds two years, the first two years must be filed separately. After the first two-year account is adjudicated, subsequent account periods may not be for more than three (3) years and each three-year period must be filed separately. [LRSF 14.34 A.] b. All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. If two or more persons join in a pleading, it may be verified by any of them. [CRC 7.103]. c. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] d. On Schedule D disbursements Executor must explain and provide the date of the court order authorizing legal fees for each entry and the case number. e. Executor must give 15 days' notice of the petition and include the required language found in LRSF 14.1 D. 6).

Trusts require 30 days' notice. Paragraph 13 of this amended petition indicates that notice will be given to the DHCS, but it was not. (The trustee does not need to give notice to himself.) rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEOLA J. WASHINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/7/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: An amended pleading completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3(3). The "amended supplement" filed on 8/25/22 is incomplete. Furthermore, no courtesy copy of the supplement filed on 8/23/22 was delivered to or received by the assigned examiner. No proof of service of notice of the "amended supplement" has been given. Has a personal representative been appointed for the other post-deceased beneficiary, Leona J. Salter? The amended supplement does not address this. No letters appointing that personal representative appears to have been filed. The executor continued payments out of the estate for properties in Louisiana the decedent did not own (four listed in paragraph 16 of the amended supplemental filed on 8/25/22, not three). Whether or not she did this during the decedent's lifetime as agent under the decedent's power of attorney, as the executor of the decedent's will, her duty now is to the beneficiaries of this estate. Such payments should be reflected as accounts receivable from those persons who actually own the properties. What has the attorney in Louisiana advised her and how will this estate recover those payments? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/28/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MIKHAIL M. VAYNBERG AND BEATRISA VAYNBERG LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Substitution Of Party
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. This trust proceeding focuses on the Mikhail M. Vaynberg and Beatrisa Vaynberg Living Trust, more specifically, Trust B. Mikhail is a settlor and the former trustee of Trust B. Co-settlor Beatrisa died in 2008. Upon Beatrisa's death, the trust was divided into a survivor's trust, known as Trust A, and a marital trust, known as Trust B. Trust B is, and at all relevant times has been, irrevocable. Alexander Vaynberg is Mikhail and Beatrisa's son and a beneficiary of Trust B. Pending are two substantive petitions: (1) Mikhail's Petition for Order Determining Interest in Trust Property, Directing Trustees to Transfer Possession of Property, Accounting, Damages, Attorney Fees and Costs, filed September 23, 2020, as amended in March 2021 ("Mikhail's Petition"); and (2) Alexander's Petition to Remove Trustee, for Accounting, and for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, filed November 24, 2020 ("Alexander's Petition"). The court suspended Mikhail as trustee and, pursuant to stipulation, appointed private fiduciary Minna Fernan as sole interim trustee. Mikhail has since resigned as trustee. The court ordered Mikhail and Alexander, separately, to account. By his petition, Mikhail seeks an order directing Alexander, who apparently collected rents from real property held by Trust B to account for the rents and submit them to the Trustee. By his petition, Alexander seeks an order removing Mikhail as trustee, directing Mikhail to account back to 2006 and finding Mikhail breached the Trust. By the instant motion, Mikhail seeks an order allowing Yafim Ratinski, as the attorney-in-fact for Mikhail in his individual capacity pursuant to an April 8, 2019 General Durable Power of Attorney, to substitute in for Mikhail as former trustee of Trust B. The legitimacy and enforceability of documents executed by Mikhail in and around April 2019 are disputed. Mikhail's motion is DENIED. "An action or proceeding does not abate by the disability of a party. The court, on motion, shall allow the action or proceeding to be continued by or against the party's representative." (Code Civ. Proc., section 375.) "Disability" in this context includes legal incapacity as well as death of a party. (See, e.g., Cal. Civ. Prac. Procedure section 3:115 (Form Order substituting representative for disabled party) Of course, the rule for substitution assumes that the disabled party, particularly where he is the petitioner, possessed legal capacity when his operative pleading was filed. The parties do not appear to dispute the validity of Mikhail's October 2, 2021 progressive dementia diagnosis, or Mikhail's contention that he is "disabled" for purposes of the statute (Lvovich Decl. Exh. 5, certified translation Exh. 6). Indeed, since July 2019, Alexander has been arguing to this court that his father lacks legal capacity; that's what triggered Alexander's Petition for Appointment of Probate Conservator of the Estate of Mikhail Vaynberg in case number PCN-19-302986. What is in dispute on this motion is who, if anyone, should be appointed to represent the interests of Mikhail as former trustee of the B Trust. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MIKHAIL M. VAYNBERG AND BEATRISA VAYNBERG LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Substitution Of Party (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Where a former trustee lacks the capacity to verify an accounting, who can step in? It is widely understood that the former trustee's conservator could execute the accounting. (See Prob. C., sections 10953, subds. (c) & (d); 2632, subds. (c) & (d); 17206; see also CEB, California Trust Administration, section 4.47.) But, here, no conservatorship is in place, nor is it likely a cognizable conservatorship will be established, given that Mikhail has been living in Israel since approximately 2019 and it does not appear that he intends to return to California. Alternatively, the court may order the attorney for the unavailable principle to execute an account. (See Prob. C., sections 10953, subds. (c) & (d); 2632, subds. (c) & (d); 17206.) The court is not inclined to appoint Yafim to represent the former trustee at this time, given that the request is based on a disputed DPOA and a viable alternative to his appointment exists. Past the accountings, it is unclear to the court whether Mikhail's Petition remains cognizable given that he brought his claims as trustee [see Mikhail's Pet., p. 1] and he is no longer trustee. It is also unclear to the court whether Mikhail had capacity in March 2021 when he filed his Amened Petition (or even September 2020 when he filed his Initial Petition). If Mikhail's Petition remains cognizable, once the accountings have been completed, the parties and the court can determine who can represent the former trustee's interests in (a) the trustee's claims against Alexander and (b) Alexander's breach-of-trust claims against the former trustee. Mikhail's request that the court disregard Alexander's untimely opposition is denied. The court offered Mikhail an extension of time to file a reply and the offer was denied. Mikhail filed a substantive reply, and he has failed to show any prejudice from Alexander's untimely filing. Alexander, however, is advised that future breaches are not likely to be met with the same response. All parties are expected to be familiar with, and comply with, all applicable procedural and substantive rules. Alexander is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MIKHAIL M. VAYNBERG AND BEATRISA VAYNBERG LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Substitution Of Party (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] Mikhail basis his request that Yafim replace him on the notion that Yafim is Mikhail's personal representative under the April 2019 DPOA. The moving party argues that this court validated the DPOA in its January 17, 2020 Order Denying Alexander's Conservatorship Petition. To the extent Mikhail argues that the January 2020 Order is preclusive, he has forfeited the argument by presenting no legal argument or meaningful analysis supporting preclusion-whether by res judicata or collateral estoppel. In any event the conservatorship order is not preclusive on the validity of the April 2019 DPOA. The conservatorship court made only two findings: "1. All notices required by law have been given. 2. Alexander failed to establish by clear and convincing evidence that proposed conservatee is substantially unable to manage his own financial resources or resist fraud or undue influence pursuant to Probate Code section 1801." The court has never made any determination regarding the legitimacy or enforceability of the April 2019 DPOA. In any event, the court declines to rely on the April 2019 DPOA to substitute Yafim for Mikhail in his capacity as trustee of the B Trust. The DPOA does not make substituting Yafim mandatory. An agent may hold a power of attorney to act on behalf of a principal as to the principal's personal financial matters, but it does not follow that where the principal is a duly appointed trustee of a trust, the agent may assume the role of trustee too. The principal, himself, must exercise his trustee duties. (See Rest.3d, Trusts, section 80 ["A trustee has a duty to perform the responsibilities of the trusteeship personally, except as a prudent person of comparable skill might delegate those responsibilities to others."].) If he cannot personally perform his trustee duties, then he must resign or be removed. Appointment of a successor trustee depends on the provisions of the trust instrument and the relevant law. But finding that an agent has a right to assume the role of trustee or perform tasks required by the trustee or former trustee simply because he holds a power of attorney authorizing him to act as to the principal's agent as to his personal financial affairs would be to allow the POA holder to gain control of a trust and trust property irrespective of the settlors' intent, the trust instrument, or the provisions for appointment of trustees and amendment of the trust. This result would run contrary to basic trust law. (See Rest.3d, Trusts, section 80, com. (c ) ["A trustee cannot properly commit the entire administration of the trust to an agent or other person, except as permitted to do so by the terms of the trust."].) And, on this record, the court declines to exercise its discretion to substitute Yafim for Mikhail as former trustee, given the disputed status of the April 2019 DPOA (upon which the request is based) and the absence of any showing that Yafim could, for example, competently and meaningfully verify a trustee accounting. [Part 2 of 3]

For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARVIN EDWARDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/7/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The petitioner is also reportedly the successor trustee of the Marvin Edwards Trust. Who are the trust beneficiaries? Donald Leonard is missing from paragraph 29 of the report. Is he not entitled to notice? After notice issues are addressed, an appearance may be required for the numerous hours spent on drafting the initial petitions and also this fee petition. (Separate fee declarations setting forth all of the detailed time entries again are not necessary when time sheets are submitted as part of a petition for fees.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/28/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANK MON GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/23/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please provide legal authority regarding a California Court having authority to transfer an Oregon Permit. Also, the provided language says "his or her permit may then be purchased from the personal representative or executor administering the estate of the permit holder." The provided document does not state anything about a transfer without a purchase. A file stamped single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/7/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIAL LANDHERR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/5/22. No earlier date available. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No other documents filed in support of the petition have been filed: proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate; proof of publication (Taos News is not a San Francisco newspaper [Probate Code Section 8121(b).]); Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities; Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S), which must be filed conventionally and confidentially with a cover sheet. Item 2 of the petition is checked, indicating that the petitioner is seeking to have a will admitted. A non-resident personal representative will be required to post bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] No waivers of bond are attached to the petition, as alleged in item 3e(3). The permanent address of the proposed personal representative is not just the state. Items 6 and 7 of the petition should not be answered if issue survived. The proposed order and courtesy copies of all supporting documents as well as a verified supplement to the petition, addressing the issues with the petition noted here, must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 11/22/22 to avoid another automatic continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENNETH R. MCLEA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/23/2022 at request of counsel. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a file stamped courtesy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. File stamped courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. A hard copy of the proposed order must be provided to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.
